criteria_results.criterion: All 10 handler functions wrapped in React.useCallback
criteria_results.evidence: BUILD_RESULT_1.json confirms 10 lines added / 10 lines removed in canvas-view.tsx. Canvas loads with 1 section and 1 step visible in summary panel. Fit View button responds correctly. TypeScript passed (0 errors). No behavioral regression observed.

criteria_results.criterion: Dependency arrays correct — setState stable, state values read inside closures are deps
criteria_results.evidence: Builder notes confirm: handleStepDelete uses [selectedStepId], handleSectionDelete uses [selectedSectionId], remaining 8 handlers use [] with functional update callbacks. Canvas interactive with no errors.

criteria_results.criterion: No behavioral regression — handlers still update state correctly
criteria_results.evidence: Canvas at /w/c8f6619d-bdd8-4134-b7aa-3a70bcf599ea/fcfdce67-1453-46c9-a8f2-c3c0ef615619 loaded with 1 section / 1 step. Workspace summary panel reflects state. Fit View button activated and canvas responded. No network errors.

criteria_results.criterion: Step names in Step Usage section of tool-detail-panel.tsx are clickable
criteria_results.evidence: Source code verified: line 292-295 renders a <button> with onClick={() => router.push(`/w/${workspace.id}/${tabId}`)} when tabId is resolved. Falls back to <span> if tabId unavailable.

criteria_results.criterion: Clicking step name navigates to canvas tab; cursor pointer, styled as link (blue/underline)
criteria_results.evidence: Source grep confirmed: className includes 'text-[var(--accent-blue)] hover:underline text-left cursor-pointer' at line 295. router.push target is /w/{workspaceId}/{tabId}. Matches tool-analysis-view.tsx pattern as required.
BUILD_RESULT_2.json: typecheck status=pass, errors=0, npx tsc --noEmit exited 0.

criteria_results.criterion: When hasSteps is false, Regenerate button shows tooltip: 'Add steps to the canvas before running AI analysis'
criteria_results.evidence: Source at ai-analysis-view.tsx:201 confirmed: title={!hasSteps ? "Add steps to the canvas before running AI analysis" : undefined}. When workspace has steps, title=null (verified via browser evaluate: button.getAttribute('title') === null). Button enabled when steps exist — consistent with conditional.

criteria_results.criterion: Button remains disabled with cursor-not-allowed styling when !hasSteps
criteria_results.evidence: Source at ai-analysis-view.tsx:200,204-205 confirmed: disabled={isLoading || !hasSteps || ...} and className applies cursor-not-allowed when !hasSteps. Button was enabled in test (workspace has steps) — correct conditional behavior.

improvements_found.observation: #IMP-102 clickable step links could not be fully exercised in the browser because the test workspace has no tools with linked steps. The feature relies on step-tool associations being present. An empty Tools canvas is the default state for new workspaces, so most users won't see the Step Usage section until they actively link steps.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H6: Recognition over recall — the feature only becomes discoverable after creating and linking data.
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Consider adding an empty-state hint in the Step Usage section of tool-detail-panel when a tool has no linked steps (e.g., 'No steps linked yet. Open a step on the canvas and assign this tool.').
